title: Johnson & Johnson — ao_plan_dave
html:
J&J — Dave Johnson AO Perspective (FY26)

Source: Dave Johnson FY26 AO Plan (Apr 2026 update) AO: Dave Johnson (Principal) Total J&J FY25 Base: $4.5M Total J&J FY26 Target: $5.0M (12% growth) FY26 YTD (3/31/26): $4.1M (82% of target)

Structure

Segment AD FY25 Base FY26 Target FY26 YTD
J&J Oncology Anna Chubaeva $3.6M $4.0M (+10%) $3.3M (84%)
J&J Non-Oncology Sara Farina $0.87M $1.05M (+20%) $927K (89%)
J&J Rebates — -$220K -$250K -$140K
Total $4.5M $5.0M $4.1M

FY26 Priorities (Account-wide)

  • Seek broader opportunities across J&J (AI, cross-portfolio tracking, HCP-Patient)
  • Elevate partnership at Director/above level
  • Provide more strategic guidance / consulting
  • Gain introduction to Jesse Heap (AI Lead)
  • Broaden relationship with Igor Rudychev for cross-brand analytics

1H26 Key Milestones Achieved

  • Meeting with Kevin O'Neil (FY1Q) — precursor to greater oversight, increased consistency, stronger KPI focus, AI exploration
  • Portfolio-wide MSL win for >$1.0M in bookings — driving significant growth in non-onc and onc; surveys going live 4/2/26
  • AI RFP / Hackathon launched late 1H26 — potential to greatly shape account in FY26 and beyond
  • Strong oncology renewals (~$2.8M) including expanding INLEXZO launch work
  • FRM pipeline for oncology + immunology + potential neuroscience (2H26)
  • Good connections from PMRC, including global PET opportunity

Financial position:

  • Current bookings: $4.1M
  • Weighted pipeline: $423K
  • Gap to $5.0M target: $475K
  • Forecast: Expect to achieve (or be very close to) $5M goal

J&J Oncology Priorities (Anna)

  • Full renewals of all PETs, particularly PET extensions
  • Build awareness/value proposition for media tracking, AI, new offerings
  • Elevate discussions with portfolio opportunities
  • Key milestones: First ATU win on oncology (MM); 2H26 Legend/J&J co-promote PET for CARVYKTI; HNSCC expansion for Rybrevant PET; CCA pilot results for prostate

J&J Non-Oncology Priorities (Sara)

  • Gain renewals of PET, HCP-Patient, MSL, FRM, KAM tracking
  • Expand opportunities (AI, ATU, SFTO, pilots)
  • Gain introductions to new teams (global)
  • Key milestones: Continue MSL expansion (1Q-2Q); IMAAVY (gMG) PET opportunity (2H26); expand FRM/MSL; refocus on Lisa Gaynor for broader capabilities
  • Key relationships: Jessica Leinert, Liz Beal, Pam Trainer, Payal (MSL), Devon (FRM)

J&J AI Landscape

  • Igor Rudychev (Lead) — reporting team: Elena Brown (POC) + 8; MR team: Ben Klein (POC) + 6
  • Conducting full-scale analysis / hackathon covering wide areas of AI enablement
  • ZoomRx participating in the hackathon (began April 1)
  • Competitors identified: Trinity and IQVIA
  • Next steps likely dependent on hackathon results

Biggest Risks

  1. AI hackathon outcome (likely not impacting revenue until FY27)
  2. Increased oversight/decision-making from VP (Kevin O'Neil) over J&J Insights team

Back to Accounts Hub | Home
